tEn: Mobile ALOHA: Learning Bimanual Mobile Manipulation with Low-Cost Whole-Body Teleoperation
tZh: Mobile ALOHA：通过低成本全身遥操作学习双臂移动操作
sEn: Mobile ALOHA enables bimanual mobile manipulation via low-cost whole-body teleoperation and imitation learning, achieving high success rates on complex tasks.
sZh: Mobile ALOHA通过低成本全身遥操作和模仿学习实现双臂移动操作，在复杂任务上取得高成功率。
absEn: Imitation learning from human demonstrations has shown impressive performance in robotics. However, most results focus on table-top manipulation, lacking the mobility and dexterity necessary for generally useful tasks. In this work, we develop a system for imitating mobile manipulation tasks that are bimanual and require whole-body control. We first present Mobile ALOHA, a low-cost and whole-body teleoperation system for data collection. It augments the ALOHA system with a mobile base, and a whole-body teleoperation interface. Using data collected with Mobile ALOHA, we then perform supervised behavior cloning and find that co-training with existing static ALOHA datasets boosts performance on mobile manipulation tasks. With 50 demonstrations for each task, co-training can increase success rates by up to 90%, allowing Mobile ALOHA to autonomously complete complex mobile manipulation tasks such as sauteing and serving a piece of shrimp, opening a two-door wall cabinet to store heavy cooking pots, calling and entering an elevator, and lightly rinsing a used pan using a kitchen faucet. Project website: https://mobile-aloha.github.io
absZh: 从人类演示中进行的模仿学习在机器人领域展现了令人印象深刻的表现。然而，大多数结果集中在桌面操作上，缺乏完成一般有用任务所需的移动性和灵巧性。在这项工作中，我们开发了一个系统，用于模仿需要全身控制的双臂移动操作任务。我们首先提出了Mobile ALOHA，一个用于数据收集的低成本全身遥操作系统。它在ALOHA系统的基础上增加了移动基座和全身遥操作界面。利用Mobile ALOHA收集的数据，我们随后进行监督行为克隆，并发现与现有的静态ALOHA数据集共同训练可以提升移动操作任务的性能。对于每个任务，使用50个演示，共同训练可以将成功率提高高达90%，使Mobile ALOHA能够自主完成复杂的移动操作任务，例如翻炒和盛出一只虾、打开双门壁柜存放沉重的烹饪锅、呼叫并进入电梯，以及使用厨房水龙头轻轻冲洗用过的平底锅。项目网站：https://mobile-aloha.github.io

contrib.en: Presents Mobile ALOHA, a low-cost whole-body teleoperation system for bimanual mobile manipulation data collection.
contrib.zh: 提出了Mobile ALOHA，一种低成本全身遥操作系统，用于双臂移动操作数据收集。

contrib.en: Shows that co-training with static ALOHA datasets boosts mobile manipulation success rates by up to 90% with 50 demonstrations.
contrib.zh: 展示了与静态ALOHA数据集联合训练，在50次示范下可将移动操作成功率提升高达90%。

contrib.en: Achieves over 80% success on complex tasks like opening cabinets, calling elevators, and rinsing pans with only 20-50 demonstrations.
contrib.zh: 在打开柜子、呼叫电梯和冲洗锅等复杂任务上，仅用20-50次示范即实现超过80%的成功率。

contrib.en: Demonstrates that co-training improves data efficiency and generalization across tasks and imitation learning methods.
contrib.zh: 证明了联合训练提高了数据效率和跨任务及模仿学习方法的泛化能力。

contrib.en: Provides an open-source, low-cost hardware platform ($32k) for whole-body teleoperation and autonomous operation.
contrib.zh: 提供了一个开源、低成本的硬件平台（3.2万美元），用于全身遥操作和自主操作。

contrib.en: Validates the system through extensive real-world experiments on 7 diverse mobile manipulation tasks.
contrib.zh: 通过在7个多样化的移动操作任务上进行大量真实世界实验，验证了该系统。

limits.en: Limited to single-task imitation learning; robot cannot improve autonomously or explore new knowledge.
limits.zh: 仅限于单任务模仿学习；机器人无法自主改进或探索新知识。

limits.en: Demonstrations collected by expert operators; not tested on highly suboptimal or heterogeneous datasets.
limits.zh: 示范由专家操作员收集；未在高度次优或异构数据集上测试。

limits.en: Hardware footprint (90cm x 135cm) may be too large for narrow paths; fixed arm height limits access to low cabinets.
limits.zh: 硬件占地面积（90厘米×135厘米）可能对狭窄路径过大；固定臂高限制了进入低柜的能力。

limits.en: Cook Shrimp task success rate only 40% due to limited demonstrations and visual challenges.
limits.zh: 炒虾任务成功率仅40%，由于示范有限和视觉挑战。

limits.en: Co-training with static data may not benefit retrieval-based methods like VINN due to action prediction mismatch.
limits.zh: 与静态数据联合训练可能对基于检索的方法（如VINN）无益，由于动作预测不匹配。

items.en: Imitation learning from human demonstrations has shown impressive performance in robotics. However, most results focus on table-top manipulation, lacking the mobility and dexterity necessary for generally useful tasks. In this work, we develop a system for imitating mobile manipulation tasks that are bimanual and require whole-body control. We first present Mobile ALOHA, a low-cost and whole-body teleoperation system for data collection. It augments the ALOHA system [104] with a mobile base, and a whole-body teleoperation interface. Using data collected with Mobile ALOHA, we then perform supervised behavior cloning and find that co-training with existing static ALOHA datasets boosts performance on mobile manipulation tasks. With 50 demonstrations for each task, co-training can increase success rates by up to 90%, allowing Mobile ALOHA to autonomously complete complex mobile manipulation tasks such as sauteing and serving a piece of shrimp, opening a two-door wall cabinet to store heavy cooking pots, calling and entering an elevator, and lightly rinsing a used pan using a kitchen faucet.
items.zh: 来自人类演示的模仿学习在机器人领域展现了令人印象深刻的表现。然而，大多数结果集中在桌面操作上，缺乏通用任务所需的移动性和灵巧性。在这项工作中，我们开发了一个用于模仿需要双臂和全身控制的移动操作任务的系统。我们首先提出了Mobile ALOHA，一种低成本、全身遥操作的数据采集系统。它通过移动基座和全身遥操作接口增强了ALOHA系统[104]。利用Mobile ALOHA收集的数据，我们随后执行监督行为克隆，并发现与现有的静态ALOHA数据集共同训练能提升移动操作任务的性能。对于每个任务，使用50个演示，共同训练可以将成功率提高高达90%，使Mobile ALOHA能够自主完成复杂的移动操作任务，例如炒菜和上菜、打开双门壁柜存放沉重的烹饪锅、呼叫并进入电梯，以及用厨房水龙头轻轻冲洗用过的平底锅。

items.en: Imitation learning from human-provided demonstrations is a promising tool for developing generalist robots, as it allows people to teach arbitrary skills to robots. Indeed, direct behavior cloning can enable robots to learn a variety of primitive robot skills ranging from lane-following in mobile robots [67], to simple pick-and-place manipulation skills [12, 20] to more delicate manipulation skills like spreading pizza sauce or slotting in a battery [18, 104]. However, many tasks in realistic, everyday environments require whole-body coordination of both mobility and dexterous manipulation, rather than just individual mobility or manipulation behaviors. For example, consider the relatively basic task of putting away a heavy pot into a cabinet in Figure LABEL:fig:teaser. The robot needs to first navigate to the cabinet, necessitating the mobility of the robot base. To open the cabinet, the robot needs to back up while simultaneously maintaining a firm grasp of the two door handles, motivating whole-body control. Subsequently, both arms need to grasp the pot handles and together move the pot into the cabinet, emphasizing the importance of bimanual coordination. Along a similar vein, cooking, cleaning, housekeeping, and even simply navigating an office using an elevator all require mobile manipulation and are often made easier with the added flexibility of two arms. In this paper, we study the feasibility of extending imitation learning to tasks that require whole-body control of bimanual mobile robots.
items.zh: 来自人类提供的演示的模仿学习是开发通用机器人的一种有前景的工具，因为它允许人们向机器人教授任意技能。事实上，直接行为克隆可以使机器人学习各种原始机器人技能，从移动机器人中的车道保持[67]，到简单的抓取和放置操作技能[12, 20]，再到更精细的操作技能，如涂抹披萨酱或插入电池[18, 104]。然而，现实日常环境中的许多任务需要移动性和灵巧操作的全身协调，而不仅仅是单独的移动或操作行为。例如，考虑图LABEL:fig:teaser中将沉重的锅放入橱柜这一相对基本的任务。机器人需要首先导航到橱柜，这需要机器人基座的移动性。为了打开橱柜，机器人需要后退，同时保持牢固抓住两个门把手，这需要全身控制。随后，两只手臂需要抓住锅把手并一起将锅移入橱柜，强调了双臂协调的重要性。类似地，烹饪、清洁、家务，甚至使用电梯在办公室中导航都需要移动操作，并且通常因双臂的额外灵活性而变得更容易。在本文中，我们研究了将模仿学习扩展到需要双臂移动机器人全身控制的任务的可行性。

items.en: Two main factors hinder the wide adoption of imitation learning for bimanual mobile manipulation. (1) We lack accessible, plug-and-play hardware for whole-body teleoperation. Bimanual mobile manipulators can be costly if purchased off-the-shelf. Robots like the PR2 and the TIAGo can cost more than $200k USD, making them unaffordable for typical research labs. Additional hardware and calibration are also necessary to enable teleoperation on these platforms. For example, the PR1 uses two haptic devices for bimanual teleoperation and foot pedals to control the base [93]. Prior work [5] uses a motion capture system to retarget human motion to a TIAGo robot, which only controls a single arm and needs careful calibration. Gaming controllers and keyboards are also used for teleoperating the Hello Robot Stretch [2] and the Fetch robot [1], but do not support bimanual or whole-body teleoperation. (2) Prior robot learning works have not demonstrated high-performance bimanual mobile manipulation for complex tasks. While many recent works demonstrate that highly expressive policy classes such as diffusion models and transformers can perform well on fine-grained, multi-modal manipulation tasks, it is largely unclear whether the same recipe will hold for mobile manipulation: with additional degrees of freedom added, the interaction between the arms and base actions can be complex, and a small deviation in base pose can lead to large drifts in the arm’s end-effector pose. Overall, prior works have not delivered a practical and convincing solution for bimanual mobile manipulation, both from a hardware and a learning standpoint.
items.zh: 阻碍模仿学习在双臂移动操作中广泛采用的两个主要因素是：（1）我们缺乏可访问的、即插即用的全身遥操作硬件。双臂移动操作机器人如果现成购买可能成本高昂。像PR2和TIAGo这样的机器人成本可能超过20万美元，使得典型研究实验室难以承受。还需要额外的硬件和校准才能在这些平台上实现遥操作。例如，PR1使用两个触觉设备进行双臂遥操作，并使用脚踏板控制基座[93]。先前的工作[5]使用动作捕捉系统将人体运动重定向到TIAGo机器人，这仅控制单臂且需要仔细校准。游戏控制器和键盘也用于遥操作Hello Robot Stretch [2]和Fetch机器人[1]，但不支持双臂或全身遥操作。（2）先前的机器人学习工作尚未展示出针对复杂任务的高性能双臂移动操作。虽然许多近期工作表明，高度表达性的策略类别（如扩散模型和Transformer）可以在细粒度、多模态操作任务上表现良好，但尚不清楚相同的方案是否适用于移动操作：随着自由度的增加，手臂和基座动作之间的交互可能很复杂，基座姿态的微小偏差可能导致手臂末端执行器姿态的大幅漂移。总体而言，先前的工作尚未从硬件和学习角度提供实用且令人信服的双臂移动操作解决方案。

items.en: We seek to tackle the challenges of applying imitation learning to bimanual mobile manipulation in this paper. On the hardware front, we present Mobile ALOHA, a low-cost and whole-body teleoperation system for collecting bimanual mobile manipulation data. Mobile ALOHA extends the capabilities of the original ALOHA , the low-cost and dexterous bimanual puppeteering setup [104], by mounting it on a wheeled base. The user is then physically tethered to the system and backdrives the wheels to enable base movement. This allows for independent movement of the base while the user has both hands controlling ALOHA . We record the base velocity data and the arm puppeteering data at the same time, forming a whole-body teleoperation system.
items.zh: 我们试图在本文中解决将模仿学习应用于双臂移动操作的挑战。在硬件方面，我们提出了Mobile ALOHA，一种用于收集双臂移动操作数据的低成本全身遥操作系统。Mobile ALOHA通过将原始ALOHA（低成本灵巧双臂傀儡操作装置[104]）安装在轮式基座上，扩展了其能力。用户随后物理连接到系统，并通过反向驱动轮子实现基座移动。这使得基座可以独立移动，同时用户双手控制ALOHA。我们同时记录基座速度数据和手臂傀儡操作数据，形成全身遥操作系统。

items.en: On the imitation learning front, we observe that simply concatenating the base and arm actions then training via direct imitation learning can yield strong performance. Specifically, we concatenate the 14-DoF joint positions of ALOHA with the linear and angular velocity of the mobile base, forming a 16-dimensional action vector. This formulation allows Mobile ALOHA to benefit directly from previous deep imitation learning algorithms, requiring almost no change in implementation. To further improve the imitation learning performance, we are inspired by the recent success of pre-training and co-training on diverse robot datasets, while noticing that there are few to none accessible bimanual mobile manipulation datasets. We thus turn to leveraging data from static bimanual datasets, which are more abundant and easier to collect, specifically the static ALOHA datasets from [104, 81] through the RT-X release [20]. It contains 825 episodes with tasks disjoint from the Mobile ALOHA tasks, and has different mounting positions of the two arms. Despite the differences in tasks and morphology, we observe positive transfer in nearly all mobile manipulation tasks, attaining equivalent or better performance and data efficiency than policies trained using only Mobile ALOHA data. This observation is also consistent across different class of state-of-the-art imitation learning methods, including ACT [104] and Diffusion Policy [18].
items.zh: 在模仿学习方面，我们观察到简单地将基座和手臂动作拼接起来，然后通过直接模仿学习训练，可以产生强大的性能。具体来说，我们将ALOHA的14自由度关节位置与移动基座的线速度和角速度拼接起来，形成一个16维的动作向量。这种公式化使得Mobile ALOHA可以直接受益于先前的深度模仿学习算法，几乎不需要改变实现。为了进一步提高模仿学习性能，我们受到近期在多样化机器人数据集上预训练和共同训练成功的启发，同时注意到几乎没有可访问的双臂移动操作数据集。因此，我们转向利用静态双臂数据集中的数据，这些数据更丰富且更容易收集，特别是通过RT-X发布[20]的来自[104, 81]的静态ALOHA数据集。它包含825个片段，任务与Mobile ALOHA任务不重叠，并且双臂的安装位置不同。尽管任务和形态存在差异，但我们观察到几乎所有移动操作任务中都存在正迁移，达到了与仅使用Mobile ALOHA数据训练的策略相当或更好的性能和更高的数据效率。这一观察结果在包括ACT [104]和扩散策略[18]在内的不同类别的先进模仿学习方法中也是一致的。

items.en: The main contribution of this paper is a system for learning complex mobile bimanual manipulation tasks. Core to this system is both (1) Mobile ALOHA, a low-cost whole-body teleoperation system, and (2) the finding that a simple co-training recipe enables data-efficient learning of complex mobile manipulation tasks. Our teleoperation system is capable of multiple hours of consecutive usage, such as cooking a 3-course meal, cleaning a public bathroom, and doing laundry. Our imitation learning result also holds across a wide range of complex tasks such as opening a two-door wall cabinet to store heavy cooking pots, calling an elevator, pushing in chairs, and cleaning up spilled wine. With co-training, we are able to achieve over 80% success on these tasks with only 50 human demonstrations per task, with an average of 34% absolute improvement compared to no co-training.
items.zh: 本文的主要贡献是一个用于学习复杂移动双臂操作任务的系统。该系统的核心是（1）Mobile ALOHA，一种低成本全身遥操作系统，以及（2）发现简单的共同训练方案能够实现复杂移动操作任务的数据高效学习。我们的遥操作系统能够连续使用数小时，例如烹饪三道菜、清洁公共浴室和洗衣服。我们的模仿学习结果也适用于各种复杂任务，例如打开双门壁柜存放沉重的烹饪锅、呼叫电梯、推椅子和清理洒出的红酒。通过共同训练，我们能够在每个任务仅使用50个人类演示的情况下实现超过80%的成功率，与没有共同训练相比，平均绝对改进为34%。

Middle: The teleoperation setup can be removed and only two ViperX 300 [3] are used during autonomous execution. Both arms can reach a min/max height of 65cm/200cm, and extends 100cm from the base. Right: Technical specifications of Mobile ALOHA .

items.en: Many current mobile manipulation systems utilize model-based control, which involves integrating human expertise and insights into the system’s design and architecture [52, 17, 33, 9, 93]. A notable example of model-based control in mobile manipulation is the DARPA Robotics Challenge [56]. Nonetheless, these systems can be challenging to develop and maintain, often requiring substantial team efforts, and even minor errors in perception modeling can result in significant control failures [51, 6]. Recently, learning-based approaches have been applied to mobile manipulation, alleviating much of the heavy engineering. In order to tackle the exploration problem in high-dimensional state and action spaces of mobile manipulation tasks, prior works use predefined skill primitives [86, 91, 92], reinforcement learning with decomposed action spaces [94, 48, 38, 101, 58], or whole-body control objectives [36, 42, 99]. Unlike these prior works that use action primitives, state estimators, depth images or object bounding boxes, imitation learning allows mobile manipulators to learn end-to-end by directly mapping raw RGB observations to whole-body actions, showing promising results through large-scale training using real-world data [12, 4, 78] in indoor environments [39, 78]. Prior works use expert demonstrations collected by using a VR interface [76], kinesthetic teaching [100], trained RL policies [43], a smartphone interface [90], motion capture systems [5], or from humans [8]. Prior works also develop humanoid teleoperation by using human motion capture suits [23, 22, 19, 26], exoskeleton [32, 45, 72, 75], VR headsets for visual feedbacks [15, 65, 53, 87], and haptic feedback devices [14, 66]. Purushottam et al. develop an exoskeleton suit attached to a force plate for whole-body teleoperation of a wheeled humanoid, However, there is no low-cost solution to collecting whole-body expert demonstrations for bimanual mobile manipulation. We present Mobile ALOHA for this problem. It is suitable for hour-long teleoperation, and does not require a FPV goggle for streaming back videos from the robot’s egocentric camera or haptic devices.
items.zh: 许多当前的移动操作系统采用基于模型的控制，这涉及将人类专业知识和见解整合到系统的设计和架构中[52, 17, 33, 9, 93]。移动操作中基于模型控制的一个显著例子是DARPA机器人挑战赛[56]。尽管如此，这些系统可能难以开发和维护，通常需要大量的团队努力，并且感知建模中的微小错误可能导致严重的控制失败[51, 6]。最近，基于学习的方法已应用于移动操作，减轻了许多繁重的工程工作。为了解决移动操作任务高维状态和动作空间中的探索问题，先前的工作使用了预定义的技能基元[86, 91, 92]、具有分解动作空间的强化学习[94, 48, 38, 101, 58]或全身控制目标[36, 42, 99]。与这些使用动作基元、状态估计器、深度图像或对象边界框的先前工作不同，模仿学习允许移动操作机器人通过直接将原始RGB观测映射到全身动作来进行端到端学习，通过使用真实世界数据的大规模训练[12, 4, 78]在室内环境[39, 78]中显示出有希望的结果。先前的工作使用通过VR界面[76]、动觉教学[100]、训练的RL策略[43]、智能手机界面[90]、动作捕捉系统[5]或来自人类[8]收集的专家演示。先前的工作还通过使用人体动作捕捉服[23, 22, 19, 26]、外骨骼[32, 45, 72, 75]、用于视觉反馈的VR头显[15, 65, 53, 87]和触觉反馈设备[14, 66]开发了人形机器人遥操作。Purushottam等人开发了一种连接到力板的外骨骼服，用于轮式人形机器人的全身遥操作。然而，目前还没有低成本的解决方案来收集双臂移动操作的全身专家演示。我们为此提出了Mobile ALOHA。它适合长时间遥操作，并且不需要FPV护目镜来流式传输机器人自我中心摄像头的视频或触觉设备。

items.en: Imitation learning enables robots to learn from expert demonstrations [67]. Behavioral cloning (BC) is a simple version, mapping observations to actions. Enhancements to BC include incorporating history with various architectures [59, 77, 47, 12], new training objectives [35, 63, 18, 104, 10], regularization [71], motor primitives [44, 64, 97, 7, 55, 62], and data preprocessing [81]. Prior works also focus on multi-task or few-shot imitation learning, [27, 46, 25, 34, 102, 50, 88, 30], language-conditioned imitation learning [82, 83, 47, 12], imitation from play data [89, 57, 21, 74], using human videos [96, 24, 84, 80, 16, 29, 60, 69], and using task-specific structures [103, 49, 83]. Scaling up these algorithms has led to systems adept at generalizing to new objects, instructions, or scenes [28, 47, 12, 54, 13]. Recently, co-training on diverse real-world datasets collected from different but similar types of robots have shown promising results on single-arm manipulation [20, 11, 98, 61, 31], and on navigation [79]. In this work, we use a co-training pipeline for bimanual mobile manipulation by leveraging the existing static bimanual manipulation datasets, and show that our co-training pipeline improves the performance and data efficiency of mobile manipulation policies across all tasks and several imitation learning methods. To our knowledge, we are the first to find that co-training with static manipulation datasets improves the performance and data efficiency of mobile manipulation policies.
items.zh: 模仿学习使机器人能够从专家演示中学习[67]。行为克隆（BC）是一种简单版本，将观测映射到动作。BC的增强包括使用各种架构引入历史信息[59, 77, 47, 12]、新的训练目标[35, 63, 18, 104, 10]、正则化[71]、运动基元[44, 64, 97, 7, 55, 62]和数据预处理[81]。先前的工作还关注多任务或少样本模仿学习[27, 46, 25, 34, 102, 50, 88, 30]、语言条件模仿学习[82, 83, 47, 12]、从游戏数据中模仿[89, 57, 21, 74]、使用人类视频[96, 24, 84, 80, 16, 29, 60, 69]以及使用任务特定结构[103, 49, 83]。扩展这些算法已经产生了擅长泛化到新物体、指令或场景的系统[28, 47, 12, 54, 13]。最近，在来自不同但相似类型机器人收集的多样化真实世界数据集上的共同训练已在单臂操作[20, 11, 98, 61, 31]和导航[79]上显示出有希望的结果。在这项工作中，我们通过利用现有的静态双臂操作数据集，为双臂移动操作使用了共同训练流程，并表明我们的共同训练流程在所有任务和几种模仿学习方法上提高了移动操作策略的性能和数据效率。据我们所知，我们是第一个发现与静态操作数据集共同训练可以提高移动操作策略的性能和数据效率的。

items.en: We develop Mobile ALOHA, a low-cost mobile manipulator that can perform a broad range of household tasks. Mobile ALOHA inherits the benefits of the original ALOHA system [104], i.e. the low-cost, dexterous, and repairable bimanual teleoperation setup, while extending its capabilities beyond table-top manipulation. Specifically, we incorporate four key design considerations:
items.zh: 我们开发了Mobile ALOHA，一种能够执行广泛家务任务的低成本移动操作机器人。Mobile ALOHA继承了原始ALOHA系统[104]的优点，即低成本、灵巧和可修复的双臂遥操作装置，同时将其能力扩展到桌面操作之外。具体来说，我们考虑了四个关键设计因素：

items.en: Mobile: The system can move at a speed comparable to human walking, around 1.42m/s.
items.zh: 移动性：系统能够以与人类步行相当的速度移动，约为1.42m/s。

items.en: Stable: It is stable when manipulating heavy household objects, such as pots and cabinets.
items.zh: 稳定性：在操作沉重的家用物品（如锅和橱柜）时保持稳定。

items.en: Whole-body teleoperation: All degrees of freedom can be teleoperated simultaneously, including both arms and the mobile base.
items.zh: 全身遥操作：所有自由度可以同时遥操作，包括双臂和移动基座。

items.en: We choose AgileX Tracer AGV ("Tracer") as the mobile base following considerations 1 and 2. Tracer is a low-profile, differential drive mobile base designed for warehouse logistics. It can move up to 1.6m/s similar to average human walking speed. With a maximum payload of 100kg and 17mm height, we can add a balancing weight low to the ground to achieve the desired tip-over stability. We found Tracer to possess sufficient traversability in accessible buildings: it can traverse obstacles as tall as 10mm and slopes as steep as 8 degrees with load, with a minimum ground clearance of 30mm. In practice, we found it capable of more challenging terrains such as traversing the gap between the floor and the elevator. Tracer costs $7,000 in the United States, more than 5x cheaper than AGVs from e.g. Clearpath with similar speed and payload.
items.zh: 我们根据考虑因素1和2选择了AgileX Tracer AGV（"Tracer"）作为移动基座。Tracer是一款低矮的差速驱动移动基座，专为仓库物流设计。它可以以高达1.6m/s的速度移动，类似于人类平均步行速度。最大有效载荷为100kg，高度为17mm，我们可以将配重低置于地面以实现所需的倾翻稳定性。我们发现Tracer在可进入的建筑物中具有足够的通过性：它可以承受高达10mm的障碍物和高达8度的斜坡（带负载），最小离地间隙为30mm。在实践中，我们发现它能够应对更具挑战性的地形，例如穿越地板和电梯之间的间隙。Tracer在美国售价为7,000美元，比具有类似速度和有效载荷的Clearpath等AGV便宜5倍以上。

items.en: We then seek to design a whole-body teleoperation system on top of the Tracer mobile base and ALOHA arms, i.e. a teleoperation system that allows simultaneous control of both the base and the two arms (consideration 3). This design choice is particularly important in household settings as it expands the available workspace of the robot. Consider the task of opening a two-door cabinet. Even for humans, we naturally step back while opening the doors to avoid collision and awkward joint configurations. Our teleoperation system shall not constrain such coordinated human motion, nor introduce unnecessary artifacts in the collected dataset. However, designing a whole-body teleoperation system can be challenging, as both hands are already occupied by the ALOHA leader arms. We found the design of tethering the operator’s waist to the mobile base to be the most simple and direct solution, as shown in Figure 1 (left). The human can backdrive the wheels which have very low friction when torqued off. We measure the rolling resistance to be around 13N on vinyl floor, acceptable to most humans. Connecting the operator to the mobile manipulator directly also enables coarse haptic feedback when the robot collides with objects. To improve the ergonomics, the height of the tethering point and the positions of the leader arms can all be independently adjusted up to 30cm. During autonomous execution, the tethering structure can also be detached by loosening 4 screws, together with the two leader arms. This reduces the footprint and weight of the mobile manipulator as shown in Figure 1 (middle). To improve the ergonomics and expand workspace, we also mount the four ALOHA arms all facing forward, different from the original ALOHA which has arms facing inward.
items.zh: 然后，我们寻求在Tracer移动基座和ALOHA手臂之上设计一个全身遥操作系统，即允许同时控制基座和双臂的遥操作系统（考虑因素3）。这一设计选择在家庭环境中尤为重要，因为它扩展了机器人的可用工作空间。考虑打开双门橱柜的任务。即使对于人类，我们也会自然地后退同时开门以避免碰撞和尴尬的关节配置。我们的遥操作系统不应限制这种协调的人类运动，也不应在收集的数据集中引入不必要的伪影。然而，设计全身遥操作系统可能具有挑战性，因为双手已经被ALOHA领导臂占用。我们发现将操作员的腰部连接到移动基座的设计是最简单直接的解决方案，如图1（左）所示。人类可以反向驱动轮子，当扭矩关闭时，轮子具有非常低的摩擦力。我们测量到在乙烯基地板上的滚动阻力约为13N，对大多数人类来说是可以接受的。将操作员直接连接到移动操作机器人还可以在机器人碰撞物体时提供粗略的触觉反馈。为了改善人体工程学，连接点的高度和领导臂的位置都可以独立调节，最多30cm。在自主执行期间，连接结构以及两个领导臂也可以通过松开4个螺丝拆卸。这减少了移动操作机器人的占地面积和重量，如图1（中）所示。为了改善人体工程学并扩展工作空间，我们还将四个ALOHA手臂全部朝前安装，这与原始ALOHA手臂朝内安装不同。

items.en: To make our mobile manipulator untethered (consideration 4), we place a 1.26kWh battery that weights 14kg at the base. It also serves as a balancing weight to avoid tipping over. All compute during data collection and inference is conducted on a consumer-grade laptop with Nvidia 3070 Ti GPU (8GB VRAM) and Intel i7-12800H. It accepts streaming from three Logitech C922x RGB webcams, at 480x640 resolution and 50Hz. Two cameras are mounted to the wrist of the follower robots, and the third facing forward. The laptop also accepts proprioception streaming from all 4 arms through USB serial ports, and from the Tracer mobile base through CAN bus. We record the linear and angular velocities of the mobile base to be used as actions of the learned policy. We also record the joint positions of all 4 robot arms to be used as policy observations and actions. We refer readers to the original ALOHA paper [104] for more details about the arms.
items.zh: 为了使我们的移动操作机器人无束缚（考虑因素4），我们在基座处放置了一个1.26kWh的电池，重14kg。它还可以作为配重，防止倾翻。数据收集和推理期间的所有计算都在一台配备Nvidia 3070 Ti GPU（8GB VRAM）和Intel i7-12800H的消费级笔记本电脑上进行。它接受来自三个Logitech C922x RGB网络摄像头的流，分辨率为480x640，频率为50Hz。两个摄像头安装在从动机器人的腕部，第三个朝前。笔记本电脑还通过USB串口接受来自所有4个手臂的本体感觉流，以及通过CAN总线接受来自Tracer移动基座的本体感觉流。我们记录移动基座的线速度和角速度，用作学习策略的动作。我们还记录所有4个机器人手臂的关节位置，用作策略的观测和动作。关于手臂的更多细节，我们请读者参考原始ALOHA论文[104]。

items.en: With design considerations above, we build Mobile ALOHA with a $32k budget, comparable to a single industrial cobot such as the Franka Emika Panda. As illustrated in Figure 1 (middle), the mobile manipulator can reach between 65cm and 200cm vertically relative to the ground, can extend 100cm beyond its base, can lift objects that weight 1.5kg, and can exert pulling force of 100N at a height of 1.5m. Some example tasks that Mobile ALOHA is capable of includes:
items.zh: 基于上述设计考虑，我们以32k美元的预算构建了Mobile ALOHA，与单个工业协作机器人（如Franka Emika Panda）相当。如图1（中）所示，移动操作机器人可以垂直达到地面以上65cm至200cm，可以超出基座延伸100cm，可以举起重达1.5kg的物体，并可以在1.5m高度施加100N的拉力。Mobile ALOHA能够执行的一些示例任务包括：

items.en: Housekeeping: Water plants, use a vacuum, load and unload a dishwasher, obtain drinks from the fridge, open doors, use washing machine, fling and spread a quilt, stuff a pillow, zip and hang a jacket, fold trousers, turn on/off a lamp, and self-charge.
items.zh: 家务：浇花、使用吸尘器、装载和卸载洗碗机、从冰箱取饮料、开门、使用洗衣机、抖开和铺平被子、填充枕头、拉上拉链并挂起夹克、折叠裤子、打开/关闭灯以及自行充电。

items.en: Cooking: Crack eggs, mince garlic, unpackage vegetables, pour liquid, sear and flip chicken thigh, blanch vegetables, stir fry, and serve food in a dish.
items.zh: 烹饪：打鸡蛋、切碎大蒜、拆开蔬菜包装、倒液体、煎烤并翻转鸡腿、焯蔬菜、炒菜以及将食物盛入盘中。

items.en: Human-robot interactions: Greet and shake “hands” with a human, open and hand a beer to human, help human shave and make bed.
items.zh: 人机交互：与人类打招呼并握手、打开啤酒并递给人类、帮助人类刮胡子和整理床铺。

items.en: We include more technical specifications of Mobile ALOHA in Figure 1 (right). Beyond the off-the-shelf robots, we open-source all of the software and hardware parts with a detailed tutorial covering 3D printing, assembly, and software installation. The tutorial is on the project website.
items.zh: 我们在图1（右）中包含了Mobile ALOHA的更多技术规格。除了现成的机器人，我们还开源了所有软件和硬件部件，并提供了涵盖3D打印、组装和软件安装的详细教程。该教程位于项目网站上。

full.secZh: 4 与静态ALOHA数据的协同训练

items.en: The typical approach for using imitation learning to solve real-world robotics tasks relies on using the datasets that are collected on a specific robot hardware platform for a targeted task. This straightforward approach, however, suffers from lengthy data collection processes where human operators collect demonstration data from scratch for every task on the a specific robot hardware platform. The policies trained on these specialized datasets are often not robust to the perceptual perturbations (e.g. distractors and lighting changes) due to the limited visual diversity in these datasets [95]. Recently, co-training on diverse real-world datasets collected from different but similar types of robots have shown promising results on single-arm manipulation [20, 61, 11, 31], and on navigation [79].
items.zh: 使用模仿学习解决现实世界机器人任务的典型方法依赖于在特定机器人硬件平台上为目标任务收集的数据集。然而，这种直接的方法存在耗时的数据收集过程，人类操作员需要为每个任务在特定的机器人硬件平台上从头开始收集演示数据。在这些专门数据集上训练的策略通常对感知扰动（例如干扰物和光照变化）不够鲁棒，因为这些数据集的视觉多样性有限[95]。最近，在不同但相似类型的机器人上收集的多样化真实世界数据集上的联合训练在单臂操作[20, 61, 11, 31]和导航[79]方面显示出有希望的结果。

items.en: In this work, we use a co-training pipeline that leverages the existing static ALOHA datasets to improve the performance of imitation learning for mobile manipulation, specifically for the bimanual arm actions. The static ALOHA datasets [104, 81] have 825 demonstrations in total for tasks including Ziploc sealing, picking up a fork, candy wrapping, tearing a paper towel, opening a plastic portion cup with a lid, playing with a ping pong, tape dispensing, using a coffee machine, pencil hand-overs, fastening a velcro cable, slotting a battery, and handling over a screw driver. Notice that the static ALOHA data is all collected on a black table-top with the two arms fixed to face towards each other. This setup is different from Mobile ALOHA where the background changes with the moving base and the two arms are placed in parallel facing the front. We do not use any special data processing techniques on either the RGB observations or the bimanual actions of the static ALOHA data for our co-training.
items.zh: 在这项工作中，我们使用联合训练流程，利用现有的静态ALOHA数据集来提高移动操作的模仿学习性能，特别是针对双臂动作。静态ALOHA数据集[104, 81]总共包含825个演示，任务包括密封Ziploc袋、拿起叉子、糖果包装、撕纸巾、打开带盖的塑料杯、玩乒乓球、胶带分配、使用咖啡机、传递铅笔、固定魔术贴电缆、插入电池和传递螺丝刀。注意，静态ALOHA数据全部是在黑色桌面上收集的，两个手臂固定并面对面。这种设置与Mobile ALOHA不同，后者的背景随移动底座变化，两个手臂平行放置并朝前。我们没有对静态ALOHA数据的RGB观测或双臂动作使用任何特殊的数据处理技术来进行联合训练。

items.en: Denote the aggregated static ALOHA data as as Dstaticsubscript𝐷staticD_{\text{static}}, and the Mobile ALOHA dataset for a task m𝑚m as Dmobilemsuperscriptsubscript𝐷mobile𝑚D_{\text{mobile}}^{m}. The bimanual actions are formulated as target joint positions aarms∈ℝ14subscript𝑎armssuperscriptℝ14a_{\text{arms}}\in\mathbb{R}^{14} which includes two continuous gripper actions, and the base actions are formulated as target base linear and angular velocities abase∈ℝ2subscript𝑎basesuperscriptℝ2a_{\text{base}}\in\mathbb{R}^{2}. The training objective for a mobile manipulation policy πmsuperscript𝜋𝑚\pi^{m} for a task m𝑚m is
items.zh: 将聚合的静态ALOHA数据记为D_static，任务m的Mobile ALOHA数据集记为D_mobile^m。双臂动作被公式化为目标关节位置a_arms ∈ ℝ^14，其中包括两个连续的夹爪动作，底座动作被公式化为目标底座线速度和角速度a_base ∈ ℝ^2。任务m的移动操作策略π^m的训练目标是

items.en: where oisuperscript𝑜𝑖o^{i} is the observation consisting of two wrist camera RGB observations, one egocentric top camera RGB observation mounted between the arms, and joint positions of the arms, and L𝐿L is the imitation loss function. We sample with equal probability from the static ALOHA data Dstaticsubscript𝐷staticD_{\text{static}} and the Mobile ALOHA data Dmobilemsuperscriptsubscript𝐷mobile𝑚D_{\text{mobile}}^{m}. We set the batch size to be 16. Since static ALOHA datapoints have no mobile base actions, we zero-pad the action labels so actions from both datasets have the same dimension. We also ignore the front camera in the static ALOHA data so that both datasets have 3 cameras. We normalize every action based on the statistics of the Mobile ALOHA dataset Dmobilemsuperscriptsubscript𝐷mobile𝑚D_{\text{mobile}}^{m} alone. In our experiments, we combine this co-training recipe with multiple base imitation learning approaches, including ACT [104], Diffusion Policy [18], and VINN [63].
items.zh: 其中o^i是观测，包括两个腕部摄像头RGB观测、一个安装在双臂之间的自顶向下中央摄像头RGB观测以及手臂的关节位置，L是模仿损失函数。我们从静态ALOHA数据D_static和Mobile ALOHA数据D_mobile^m中以相等概率采样。我们将批量大小设置为16。由于静态ALOHA数据点没有移动底座动作，我们对动作标签进行零填充，使得两个数据集的动作具有相同的维度。我们还忽略了静态ALOHA数据中的前置摄像头，以便两个数据集都有3个摄像头。我们仅基于Mobile ALOHA数据集D_mobile^m的统计信息对每个动作进行归一化。在我们的实验中，我们将这种联合训练方案与多种基础模仿学习方法结合，包括ACT [104]、扩散策略[18]和VINN [63]。

items.capEn: Figure 2: Task Definitions. We illustrate 6 real-world tasks that Mobile ALOHA can perform autonomously. The 7th task High Five is illustrated in the Appendix A.1 due to space constraint. For each task, we describe randomization and sub-task definitions. We also include an illustration of the base movement for each task (not drawn to scale).
items.capZh: 图2：任务定义。我们展示了Mobile ALOHA可以自主执行的6个真实世界任务。由于空间限制，第7个任务“击掌”在附录A.1中说明。对于每个任务，我们描述了随机化和子任务定义。我们还为每个任务包含了底座运动的示意图（未按比例绘制）。

items.en: We select 7 tasks that cover a wide range of capabilities, objects, and interactions that may appear in realistic applications. We illustrate them in Figure 2. For Wipe Wine, the robot needs to clean up spilled wine on the table. This task requires both mobility and bimanual dexterity. Specifically, the robot needs to first navigate to the faucet and pick up the towel, then navigate back to the table. With one arm lifting the wine glass, the other arm needs to wipe the table as well as the bottom of the glass with the towel. This task is not possible with static ALOHA, and would take more time for a single-armed mobile robot to accomplish. For Cook Shrimp, the robot sautes one piece of raw shrimp on both sides before serving it in a bowl. Mobility and bimanual dexterity are also necessary for this task: the robot needs to move from the stove to the kitchen island as well as flipping the shrimp with spatula while the other arm tilting the pan. This task requires more precision than wiping wine due to the complex dynamics of flipping a half-cooked shrimp. Since the shrimp may slightly stick to the pan, it is difficult for the robot to reach under the shrimp with the spatula and precisely flip it over. For Rinse Pan, the robot picks up a dirty pan and rinse it under the faucet before placing it on the drying rack. In addition to the challenges in the previous two tasks, turning on the faucet poses a hard perception challenge. The knob is made from shiny stainless steel and is small in size: roughly 4cm in length and 0.7cm in diameter. Due to the stochasticity introduced by the base motion, the arm needs to actively compensate for the errors by “visually-servoing” to the shiny knob. A centimeter-level error could result in task failure. For Use Cabinet, the robot picks up a heavy pot and places it inside a two-door cabinet. While seemingly a task that require no base movement, the robot actually needs to move back and forth four times to accomplish this task. For example when opening the cabinet door, both arms need to grasp the handle while the base is moving backward. This is necessary to avoid collision with the door and have both arms within their workspace. Maneuvers like this also stress the importance of whole-body teleoperation and control: if the arms and base control are separate, the robot will not be able to open both doors quickly and fluidly. Notably, the heaviest pot in our experiments weighs 1.4kg, exceeding the single arm’s payload limit of 750g while within the combined payload of two arms. For Call Elevator, the robot needs to enter the elevator by pressing the button. We emphasize long navigation, large randomization, and precise whole-body control in this task. The robot starts around 15m from the elevator and is randomized across the 10m wide lobby. To press the elevator button, the robot needs to go around a column and stop precisely next to the button. Pressing the button, measured 2cm×\times2cm in size, requires precision as pressing the peripheral or pressing too lightly will not activate the elevator. The robot also needs to turn sharply and precisely to enter the elevator door: there is only 30cm in clearance between the robot’s widest part and the door. For Push Chairs, the robot needs to push in 5 chairs in front of a long desk. This task emphasizes the strength of the mobile manipulator: it needs to overcome the friction between the 5kg chair and the ground with coordinated arms and base movement. To make this task more challenging, we only collect data for the first 3 chairs, and stress test the robot to extrapolate to the 4th and 5th chair. For High Five, we include illustrations in the Appendix A.1. The robot needs to go around the kitchen island, and whenever a human approach it from the front, stop moving and high five with the human. After the high five, the robot should continue moving only when the human moves out of its path. We collect data wearing different clothes and evaluate the trained policy on unseen persons and unseen attires. While this task does not require a lot of precision, it highlights Mobile ALOHA’s potential for studying human-robot interactions.
items.zh: 我们选择了7个任务，涵盖了现实应用中可能出现的广泛能力、物体和交互。我们在图2中进行了说明。对于“擦酒”，机器人需要清理桌上洒出的酒。这个任务需要移动性和双臂灵巧性。具体来说，机器人需要首先导航到水龙头并拿起毛巾，然后导航回桌子。一只手臂举起酒杯，另一只手臂需要用毛巾擦拭桌子以及杯底。这个任务对于静态ALOHA是不可能的，对于单臂移动机器人则需要更多时间。对于“炒虾”，机器人将一块生虾两面煎熟后盛入碗中。移动性和双臂灵巧性对于这个任务也是必要的：机器人需要从炉子移动到厨房岛台，同时用锅铲翻虾，另一只手臂倾斜平底锅。由于翻动半熟虾的复杂动力学，这个任务比擦酒需要更高的精度。因为虾可能会轻微粘在锅上，机器人很难用锅铲伸到虾下面并精确翻面。对于“冲洗锅”，机器人拿起脏锅在水龙头下冲洗，然后放在晾架上。除了前两个任务的挑战外，打开水龙头带来了一个困难的感知挑战。旋钮由闪亮的不锈钢制成，尺寸较小：长约4厘米，直径约0.7厘米。由于底座运动引入的随机性，手臂需要通过“视觉伺服”主动补偿误差以对准闪亮的旋钮。厘米级的误差可能导致任务失败。对于“使用橱柜”，机器人拿起一个沉重的锅并将其放入双门橱柜中。虽然看起来不需要底座移动，但机器人实际上需要来回移动四次才能完成这个任务。例如，在打开橱柜门时，两只手臂需要抓住把手，同时底座向后移动。这对于避免与门碰撞并让两只手臂都在其工作空间内是必要的。这样的操作也强调了全身遥操作和控制的重要性：如果手臂和底座控制是分离的，机器人将无法快速流畅地打开两扇门。值得注意的是，我们实验中最重的锅重1.4公斤，超过了单臂750克的负载限制，但在双臂的联合负载范围内。对于“呼叫电梯”，机器人需要通过按下按钮进入电梯。我们强调这个任务中的长距离导航、大范围随机化和精确的全身控制。机器人从距离电梯约15米处开始，并在10米宽的大厅中随机化。要按下电梯按钮，机器人需要绕过一根柱子并精确停在按钮旁边。按下尺寸为2厘米×2厘米的按钮需要精度，因为按下边缘或按得太轻不会激活电梯。机器人还需要急转弯并精确进入电梯门：机器人最宽部分与门之间只有30厘米的间隙。对于“推椅子”，机器人需要将长桌前的5把椅子推入。这个任务强调了移动操作器的力量：它需要通过协调的手臂和底座运动克服5公斤椅子与地面之间的摩擦。为了使这个任务更具挑战性，我们只收集了前3把椅子的数据，并压力测试机器人外推到第4和第5把椅子。对于“击掌”，我们在附录A.1中提供了插图。机器人需要绕过厨房岛台，当有人从前面靠近时，停止移动并与人类击掌。击掌后，机器人应仅在人类移出其路径时继续移动。我们穿着不同的衣服收集数据，并在未见过的个人和未见过的服装上评估训练好的策略。虽然这个任务不需要很高的精度，但它突出了Mobile ALOHA在研究人机交互方面的潜力。

items.en: We want to highlight that for all tasks mentioned above, open-loop replaying a demonstration with objects restored to the same configurations will achieve zero whole-task success. Successfully completing the task requires the learned policy to react close-loop and correct for those errors. We believe the source of errors during the open-loop replaying is the mobile base’s velocity control. As an example, we observe >10cm error on average when replaying the base actions for a 180 degree turn with 1m radius. We include more details about this experiment in Appendix A.4.
items.zh: 我们想强调，对于上述所有任务，将物体恢复到相同配置的演示进行开环重放将实现零整体任务成功率。成功完成任务需要学习到的策略进行闭环反应并纠正这些错误。我们认为开环重放期间的错误来源是移动底座的速度控制。例如，我们观察到在半径为1米的180度转弯中重放底座动作时，平均误差大于10厘米。我们在附录A.4中包含了关于这个实验的更多细节。

items.en: We aim to answer two central questions in our experiments. (1) Can Mobile ALOHA acquire complex mobile manipulation skills with co-training and a small amount of mobile manipulation data? (2) Can Mobile ALOHA work with different types of imitation learning methods, including ACT [104], Diffusion Policy [18], and retrieval-based VINN [63]? We conduct extensive experiments in the real-world to examine these questions.
items.zh: 我们的实验旨在回答两个核心问题。（1）Mobile ALOHA能否通过联合训练和少量移动操作数据获得复杂的移动操作技能？（2）Mobile ALOHA能否与不同类型的模仿学习方法一起工作，包括ACT [104]、扩散策略[18]和基于检索的VINN [63]？我们在真实世界中进行了广泛的实验来检验这些问题。

items.en: As a preliminary, all methods we will examine employ “action chunking” [104], where a policy predicts a sequence of future actions instead of one action at each timestep. It is already part of the method for ACT and Diffusion policy, and simple to be added for VINN. We found action chunking to be crucial for manipulation, improving the coherence of generated trajectory and reducing the latency from per-step policy inference. Action chunking also provides a unique advantage for Mobile ALOHA: handling the delay of different parts of the hardware more flexibly. We observe a delay between target and actual velocities of our mobile base, while the delay for position-controlled arms is much smaller. To account for a delay of d𝑑d steps of the mobile base, our robot executes the first k−d𝑘𝑑k-d arm actions and last k−d𝑘𝑑k-d base actions of an action chunk of length k𝑘k.
items.zh: 作为预备，我们将检查的所有方法都采用“动作分块”[104]，其中策略在每个时间步预测一系列未来动作而不是单个动作。这已经是ACT和扩散策略方法的一部分，并且可以简单地添加到VINN中。我们发现动作分块对于操作至关重要，它提高了生成轨迹的连贯性并减少了每步策略推理的延迟。动作分块还为Mobile ALOHA提供了一个独特的优势：更灵活地处理硬件不同部分的延迟。我们观察到移动底座的目标速度和实际速度之间存在延迟，而位置控制的手臂的延迟要小得多。为了应对移动底座的d步延迟，我们的机器人执行长度为k的动作块中的前k-d个手臂动作和后k-d个底座动作。

items.en: We start with ACT [104], the method introduced with ALOHA, and train it on all 7 tasks with and without co-training. We then evaluate each policy in the real-world, with randomization of robot and objects configurations as described in Figure 2. To calculate the success rate for a sub-task, we divide #​S​u​c​c​e​s​s#𝑆𝑢𝑐𝑐𝑒𝑠𝑠\#Success by #​A​t​t​e​m​p​t​s#𝐴𝑡𝑡𝑒𝑚𝑝𝑡𝑠\#Attempts. For example in the case of Lift Glass and Wipe sub-task, the #​A​t​t​e​m​p​t​s#𝐴𝑡𝑡𝑒𝑚𝑝𝑡𝑠\#Attempts equals the number of success from the previous sub-task Grasp Towel, as the robot could fail and stop at any sub-task. This also means the final success rate equals the product of all sub-task success rates. We report all success rates in Table 1. Each success rate is computed from 20 trials of evaluation, except Cook Shrimp which has 5.
items.zh: 我们从ACT [104]开始，这是与ALOHA一起引入的方法，并在所有7个任务上使用和不使用联合训练进行训练。然后我们在真实世界中评估每个策略，机器人配置和物体配置的随机化如图2所述。为了计算子任务的成功率，我们将#成功除以#尝试。例如，在“举杯”和“擦拭”子任务的情况下，#尝试等于前一个子任务“抓毛巾”的成功次数，因为机器人可能在任何子任务失败并停止。这也意味着最终成功率等于所有子任务成功率的乘积。我们在表1中报告了所有成功率。每个成功率由20次评估试验计算得出，但“炒虾”为5次。

items.en: With the help of co-training, the robot obtains 95% success for Wipe Wine, 95% success for Call Elevator, 85% success for Use Cabinet, 85% success for High Five, 80% success for Rinse Pan, and 80% success for Push Chairs. Each of these tasks only requires 50 in-domain demonstrations, or 20 in the case of High Five. The only task that falls below 80% success is Cook Shrimp (40%), which is a 75-second long-horizon task for which we only collected 20 demonstrations. We found the policy to struggle with flipping the shrimp with the spatula and pouring the shrimp inside the white bowl, which has low contrast with the white table. We hypothesize that the lower success is likely due to the limited demonstration data. Co-training improves the whole-task success rate in 5 out of the 7 tasks, with a boost of 45%, 20%, 80%, 95% and 80% respectively. For the remaining two tasks, the success rate is comparable between co-training and no co-training. We find co-training to be more helpful for sub-tasks where precise manipulation is the bottleneck, for example Press Button, Flip Shrimp, and Turn On Faucet. In all of these cases, compounding errors appear to be the main source of failure, either from the stochasticity of robot base velocity control or from rich contacts such as grasping of the spatula and making contact with the pan during Flip Shrimp. We hypothesize that the “motion prior” of grasping and approaching objects in the static ALOHA dataset still benefits Mobile ALOHA, especially given the invariances introduced by the wrist camera [41]. We also find the co-trained policy to generalize better in the case of Push Chairs and Wipe Wine. For Push Chairs, both co-training and no co-training achieve perfect success for the first 3 chairs, which are seen in the demonstrations. However, co-training performs much better when extrapolating to the 4th and 5th chair, by 15% and 89% respectively. For Wipe Wine, we observe that the co-trained policy performs better at the boundary of the wine glass randomization region. We thus hypothesize that co-training can also help prevent overfitting, given the low-data regime of 20-50 demonstrations and the expressive transformer-based policy used.
items.zh: 在联合训练的帮助下，机器人获得了“擦酒”95%的成功率，“呼叫电梯”95%的成功率，“使用橱柜”85%的成功率，“击掌”85%的成功率，“冲洗锅”80%的成功率，“推椅子”80%的成功率。这些任务每个只需要50个域内演示，或者“击掌”只需要20个。唯一低于80%成功率的任务是“炒虾”（40%），这是一个75秒的长时域任务，我们只收集了20个演示。我们发现策略在用锅铲翻虾和将虾倒入白色碗中时遇到困难，因为碗与白色桌子对比度低。我们假设较低的成功率可能是由于有限的演示数据。联合训练在7个任务中的5个提高了整体任务成功率，分别提升了45%、20%、80%、95%和80%。对于剩余的两个任务，联合训练和不联合训练的成功率相当。我们发现联合训练对于精确操作是瓶颈的子任务更有帮助，例如“按按钮”、“翻虾”和“打开水龙头”。在所有这些情况下，复合错误似乎是失败的主要来源，要么来自机器人底座速度控制的随机性，要么来自丰富的接触，例如在“翻虾”期间抓取锅铲和与锅接触。我们假设静态ALOHA数据集中抓取和接近物体的“运动先验”仍然有益于Mobile ALOHA，特别是考虑到腕部摄像头引入的不变性[41]。我们还发现联合训练的策略在“推椅子”和“擦酒”的情况下泛化得更好。对于“推椅子”，联合训练和不联合训练在前3把椅子上都达到了完美成功，这些椅子在演示中出现过。然而，当外推到第4和第5把椅子时，联合训练的表现要好得多，分别提高了15%和89%。对于“擦酒”，我们观察到联合训练的策略在酒杯随机化区域的边界处表现更好。因此，我们假设联合训练也有助于防止过拟合，考虑到20-50个演示的低数据量和所使用的表达性基于Transformer的策略。

items.capEn: Figure 3: Data efficiency. Co-training with static ALOHA data leads to better data efficiency and consistent improvements over training with Mobile ALOHA data only. Figure style credits to [70].
items.capZh: 图3：数据效率。与静态ALOHA数据的联合训练带来了更好的数据效率，并且比仅使用Mobile ALOHA数据训练有持续改进。图风格致谢[70]。

full.sec: 6.2 Compatibility with ACT, Diffusion Policy, and VINN
full.secZh: 6.2 与ACT、扩散策略和VINN的兼容性

items.en: We train two recent imitation learning methods, Diffusion Policy [18] and VINN [63], with Mobile ALOHA in addition to ACT. Diffusion policy trains a neural network to gradually refine the action prediction. We use the DDIM scheduler [85] to improve inference speed, and apply data augmentation to image observations to prevent overfitting. The co-training data pipeline is the same as ACT, and we include more training details in the Appendix A.3. VINN trains a visual representation model, BYOL [37] and uses it to retrieve actions from the demonstration dataset with nearest neighbors. We augment VINN retrieval with proprioception features and tune the relative weight to balance visual and proprioception feature importance. We also retrieve an action chunk instead of a single action and find significant performance improvement similar to Zhao et al.. For co-training, we simply co-train the BYOL encoder with the combined mobile and static data.
items.zh: 除了ACT之外，我们还使用Mobile ALOHA训练了两种最近的模仿学习方法：扩散策略[18]和VINN [63]。扩散策略训练一个神经网络来逐步细化动作预测。我们使用DDIM调度器[85]来提高推理速度，并对图像观测应用数据增强以防止过拟合。联合训练数据流程与ACT相同，我们在附录A.3中包含了更多训练细节。VINN训练一个视觉表示模型BYOL [37]，并使用它通过最近邻从演示数据集中检索动作。我们使用本体感觉特征增强VINN检索，并调整相对权重以平衡视觉和本体感觉特征的重要性。我们还检索一个动作块而不是单个动作，并发现类似于Zhao等人的显著性能提升。对于联合训练，我们简单地使用组合的移动和静态数据联合训练BYOL编码器。

items.en: In Table 2, we report co-training and no cotraining success rates on 2 real-world tasks: Wipe Wine and Push Chairs. Overall, Diffusion Policy performs similarly to ACT on Push Chairs, both obtaining 100% with co-training. For Wipe Wine, we observe worse performance with diffusion at 65% success. The Diffusion Policy is less precise when approaching the kitchen island and grasping the wine glass. We hypothesize that 50 demonstrations is not enough for diffusion given it’s expressiveness: previous works that utilize Diffusion Policy tend to train on upwards of 250 demonstrations. For VINN + Chunking, the policy performs worse than ACT or Diffusion across the board, while still reaching reasonable success rates with 60% on Push Chairs and 15% on Wipe Wine. The main failure modes are imprecise grasping on Lift Glass and Wipe as well as jerky motion when switching between chunks. We find that increasing the weight on proprioception when retrieving can improve the smoothness while at a cost of paying less attention to visual inputs. We find co-training to improve Diffusion Policy’s performance, by 30% and 20% for on Wipe Wine and Push Chairs respectively. This is expected as co-training helps address overfitting. Unlike ACT and Diffusion Policy, we observe mixed results for VINN, where co-training hurts Wipe Wine by 5% while improves Push Chairs by 20%. Only the representations of VINN are co-trained, while the action prediction mechanism of VINN does not have a way to leverage the out-of-domain static ALOHA data, perhaps explaining these mixed results.
items.zh: 在表2中，我们报告了在2个真实世界任务（“擦酒”和“推椅子”）上联合训练和不联合训练的成功率。总体而言，扩散策略在“推椅子”上的表现与ACT相似，两者在联合训练下都达到了100%。对于“擦酒”，我们观察到扩散策略的性能较差，成功率为65%。扩散策略在接近厨房岛台和抓取酒杯时不够精确。我们假设50个演示对于扩散策略来说不够，因为它的表达能力：之前使用扩散策略的工作通常训练超过250个演示。对于VINN+分块，策略的表现普遍低于ACT或扩散策略，但仍然达到了合理的成功率，“推椅子”为60%，“擦酒”为15%。主要的失败模式是在“举杯”和“擦拭”时抓取不精确，以及在块之间切换时动作抖动。我们发现增加检索时本体感觉的权重可以提高平滑性，但代价是减少对视觉输入的关注。我们发现联合训练提高了扩散策略的性能，在“擦酒”和“推椅子”上分别提高了30%和20%。这是预期的，因为联合训练有助于解决过拟合。与ACT和扩散策略不同，我们观察到VINN的结果好坏参半，联合训练使“擦酒”降低了5%，但使“推椅子”提高了20%。只有VINN的表示被联合训练，而VINN的动作预测机制没有办法利用域外的静态ALOHA数据，这可能解释了这些混合的结果。

items.en: Data Efficiency. In Figure 3, we ablate the number of mobile manipulation demonstrations for both co-training and no co-training, using ACT on the Wipe Wine task. We consider 25, 35, and 50 Mobile ALOHA demonstrations and evaluate for 20 trials each. We observe that co-training leads to better data efficiency and consistent improvements over training using only Mobile ALOHA data. With co-training, the policy trained with 35 in-domain demonstrations can outperform the no co-training policy trained with 50 in-domain demonstrations, by 20% (70% vs. 50%).
items.zh: 数据效率。在图3中，我们使用ACT在“擦酒”任务上消融了移动操作演示的数量，包括联合训练和不联合训练。我们考虑了25、35和50个Mobile ALOHA演示，并各评估20次试验。我们观察到联合训练带来了更好的数据效率，并且比仅使用Mobile ALOHA数据训练有持续改进。通过联合训练，使用35个域内演示训练的策略可以优于使用50个域内演示训练的不联合训练策略，提高了20%（70%对50%）。

items.capEn: Figure 4: Teleoperator learning curves. New users can quickly approach expert speed on an unseen tasks teleoperating Mobile ALOHA .
items.capZh: 图4：操作员学习曲线。新用户可以在未见过的任务上快速接近专家速度，操作Mobile ALOHA。

items.en: Co-training Is Robust To Different Data Mixtures. We sample with equal probability from the static ALOHA datasets and the Mobile ALOHA task dataset to form a training mini-batch in our co-training experiments so far, giving a co-training data sampling rate of roughly 50%. In Table 3, we study how different sampling strategies affect performance on the Wipe Wine task. We train ACT with 30% and 70% co-training data sampling rates in addition to 50%, then evaluate 20 trials each. We see similar performance across the board, with 95%, 95% and 90% success respectively. This experiment suggests that co-training performance is not sensitive to different data mixtures, reducing the manual tuning necessary when incorporating co-training on a new task.
items.zh: 联合训练对不同数据混合具有鲁棒性。到目前为止，在我们的联合训练实验中，我们从静态ALOHA数据集和Mobile ALOHA任务数据集中以相等概率采样以形成训练小批量，联合训练数据采样率约为50%。在表3中，我们研究了不同采样策略如何影响“擦酒”任务的性能。除了50%之外，我们还以30%和70%的联合训练数据采样率训练ACT，然后各评估20次试验。我们看到整体性能相似，成功率分别为95%、95%和90%。这个实验表明联合训练性能对不同数据混合不敏感，减少了在新任务上引入联合训练时所需的手动调整。

items.en: Co-training Outperforms Pre-training. In Table 4, we compare co-training and pre-training on the static ALOHA data. For pre-training, we first train ACT on the static ALOHA data for 10K steps and then continue training with in-domain task data. We experiment with the Wipe Wine task and observe that pre-training provides no improvements over training solely on Wipe Wine data. We hypothesize that the network forgets its experience on the static ALOHA data during the fine-tuning phase.
items.zh: 联合训练优于预训练。在表4中，我们比较了在静态ALOHA数据上的联合训练和预训练。对于预训练，我们首先在静态ALOHA数据上训练ACT 10K步，然后继续使用域内任务数据进行训练。我们在“擦酒”任务上进行实验，观察到预训练相对于仅在“擦酒”数据上训练没有提供改进。我们假设网络在微调阶段忘记了其在静态ALOHA数据上的经验。

items.en: We conduct a user study to evaluate the effectiveness of Mobile ALOHA teleoperation. Specifically, we measure how fast participants are able to learn to teleoperate an unseen task. We recruit 8 participants among computer science graduate students, with 5 females and 3 males aged 21-26. Four participants has no prior teleoperation experience, and the remaining 4 have varying levels of expertise. None of the them have used Mobile ALOHA before. We start by allowing each participant to freely interact with objects in the scene for 3 minutes. We held out all objects that will be used for the unseen tasks during this process. Next, we give each participants two tasks: Wipe Wine and Use Cabinet. An expert operator will first demonstrate the task, followed by 5 consecutive trials from the participants. We record the completion time for each trial, and plot them in Figure 4. We notice a steep decline in completion time: on average, the time it took to perform the task went from 46s to 28s for Wipe Wine (down 39%), and from 75s to 36s for Use Cabinet (down 52%). The average participant can also to approach speed of expert demonstrations after 5 trials, demonstrating the ease of use and learning of Mobile ALOHA teleoperation.
items.zh: 我们进行了一项用户研究，以评估Mobile ALOHA遥操作的有效性。具体来说，我们测量参与者学习遥操作未见过的任务的速度。我们招募了8名计算机科学研究生参与者，其中5名女性和3名男性，年龄在21-26岁之间。四名参与者没有遥操作经验，其余四名具有不同水平的专业知识。他们之前都没有使用过Mobile ALOHA。我们首先让每个参与者自由与场景中的物体互动3分钟。在此过程中，我们保留了所有将用于未见任务的物体。接下来，我们给每个参与者两个任务：“擦酒”和“使用橱柜”。专家操作员首先演示任务，然后参与者进行5次连续试验。我们记录每次试验的完成时间，并在图4中绘制。我们注意到完成时间急剧下降：平均而言，执行任务的时间从46秒下降到28秒（“擦酒”，下降39%），从75秒下降到36秒（“使用橱柜”，下降52%）。平均参与者在5次试验后也能接近专家演示的速度，展示了Mobile ALOHA遥操作的易用性和易学性。

items.en: In summary, our paper tackles both the hardware and the software aspects of bimanual mobile manipulation. Augmenting the ALOHA system with a mobile base and whole-body teleoperation allows us to collect high-quality demonstrations on complex mobile manipulation tasks. Then through imitation learning co-trained with static ALOHA data, Mobile ALOHA can learn to perform these tasks with only 20 to 50 demonstrations. We are also able to keep the system accessible, with under $32k budget including onboard power and compute, and open-sourcing on both software and hardware.
items.zh: 总之，我们的论文同时解决了双臂移动操作在硬件和软件方面的问题。通过为ALOHA系统增加移动底座和全身遥操作，我们能够收集复杂移动操作任务的高质量演示。然后通过与静态ALOHA数据联合训练的模仿学习，Mobile ALOHA仅需20到50次演示就能学会执行这些任务。我们还保持了系统的可及性，预算低于3.2万美元，包括机载电源和算力，并在软件和硬件上开源。

items.en: Despite Mobile ALOHA’s simplicity and performance, there are still limitations that we hope to address in future works. On the hardware front, we will seek to reduce the occupied area of Mobile ALOHA. The current footprint of 90cm x 135cm could be too narrow for certain paths. In addition, the fixed height of the two follower arms makes lower cabinets, ovens and dish washers challenging to reach. We are planning to add more degrees of freedom to the arms’ elevation to address this issue. On the software front, we limit our policy learning results to single task imitation learning. The robot can not yet improve itself autonomously or explore to acquire new knowledge. In addition, the Mobile ALOHA demonstrations are collected by two expert operators. We leave it to future work for tackling imitation learning from highly suboptimal, heterogeneous datasets.
items.zh: 尽管Mobile ALOHA简单且性能出色，但仍存在一些局限性，我们希望在未来工作中解决。在硬件方面，我们将寻求减少Mobile ALOHA的占地面积。目前90厘米×135厘米的占地面积可能对某些路径来说过于狭窄。此外，两个从动臂的固定高度使得难以触及较低的橱柜、烤箱和洗碗机。我们计划增加手臂升降的自由度来解决这个问题。在软件方面，我们将策略学习结果限制在单任务模仿学习上。机器人尚无法自主改进或通过探索获取新知识。此外，Mobile ALOHA的演示由两名专家操作员收集。我们将处理来自高度次优、异构数据集的模仿学习留作未来工作。
